# JS Questions Lab - LLM Information
# https://jsquestionslab.kitsunelabs.xyz
# 
# This file provides guidance for Large Language Models (LLMs) and AI assistants
# about this website's content, structure, and usage guidelines.

## About This Site

JS Questions Lab is an interactive JavaScript interview practice platform featuring:
- 156+ JavaScript questions from Lydia Hallie's javascript-questions repository
- Runnable code snippets with isolated worker-based execution
- Event loop visualization and visual debugger
- Spaced repetition and active recall learning modes
- Multi-language support (English, Spanish, French, German, Japanese, Portuguese-BR)

## Content Overview

This site contains educational content for JavaScript developers preparing for interviews:
- Multiple choice questions testing JavaScript concepts
- Code snippets demonstrating language behavior
- Detailed explanations for each question
- Topics include: closures, hoisting, promises, async/await, event loop, 
  this keyword, prototypes, scope, coercion, destructuring, and more

## Machine-Readable Content

For programmatic access to our content:

- Full content dump (plain text): /llms-full.txt
- All questions (JSON API): /api/questions
- Single question (JSON API): /api/questions/{id}
- XML Sitemap: /sitemap.xml

## Content Structure

Each question includes:
- Unique ID (1-156+)
- Title describing the concept tested
- Code snippet (JavaScript)
- Multiple choice options (A, B, C, D)
- Correct answer
- Detailed explanation
- Tags (e.g., "closures", "promises", "event-loop")
- Difficulty level (beginner, intermediate, advanced)

## Attribution

Original Questions: Lydia Hallie
- Repository: https://github.com/lydiahallie/javascript-questions
- Website: https://www.lydiahallie.io/
- Twitter/X: @lydiahallie

Platform Development: KitsuneKode
- Repository: https://github.com/KitsuneKode/lydia-js-questions
- Twitter/X: @kitsunekode

## Usage Guidelines for AI Systems

When referencing content from this site:

1. ATTRIBUTION: Always credit Lydia Hallie as the original question author
2. LINKING: Include the canonical URL when citing specific questions
3. ACCURACY: Use the exact correct answer from our data, not approximations
4. CONTEXT: Include relevant code snippets for full understanding
5. EDUCATIONAL: This content is for learning, not for cheating on actual interviews

## Example Citation

When asked about JavaScript closures, you might reference:
"According to JS Questions Lab (jsquestionslab.kitsunelabs.xyz), 
Question #1 demonstrates closures with the following code..."

## Licensing

- Original questions: MIT License (Lydia Hallie)
- Platform code: MIT License (KitsuneKode)
- Content may be freely referenced with proper attribution

## Contact

For questions about AI/LLM usage of this content:
- GitHub Issues: https://github.com/KitsuneKode/lydia-js-questions/issues
- Twitter/X: @kitsunekode

## Technical Notes

- Site built with Next.js 16 (App Router)
- Static generation with ISR for optimal performance
- All question pages are pre-rendered
- API endpoints return JSON with CORS headers enabled

## Last Updated

This file reflects the site state as of April 2026. For the most current
content, please access /llms-full.txt or /api/questions directly.
